38: **Spain**, currently fourth in Group H on 1 point from their opening draw, cannot afford another stumble when they face **Saudi Arabia**, who sit first in the group — also on 1 point — by virtue of goal difference. It's an uncomfortable early test for Luis de la Fuente's side, and with **Uruguay** vs **Cabo Verde** running simultaneously, the pressure to take all three points is immediate and unambiguous.
39: **Belgium** sit third in Group G on 1 point after being held in their opener, and a second game without a win against **IR Iran** — who are top of the group on the same 1 point — would put the Red Devils in a precarious position at this stage. Iran showed defensive resilience in their draw and will look to frustrate again, making this one of the trickiest tests the Belgians will face in the group stage.
37: **Uruguay**, second in Group H on 1 point, know that three points against **Cabo Verde** — third in the group, also on 1 point — would almost certainly lift them into a strong knockout position, given their superior squad depth and tournament pedigree. The Cape Verdeans earned their point with grit and won't be pushed around, but the South Americans carry both the quality and the urgency to impose themselves here.
40: **New Zealand** sit second in Group G on 1 point and face a **Egypt** side that are fourth on the same 1 point — though in a group where all four teams are level, those positional labels mean almost nothing yet. The All Whites will look to press high and use their physicality, but Egypt's technical quality in midfield gives them a genuine path to a result that could reshape the group entirely.
34: **Ecuador** sit third in Group E on 0 points after one game and desperately need a win against **Curaçao**, who are fourth and also pointless — but carrying a goal difference of -6 after being thrashed in their opener. La Tri's miserly defensive structure should prove too much for the tournament's smallest-ever qualifier, but with only one shot at three points in this fixture, Ecuador cannot afford complacency.
36: **Japan** are third in Group F on 1 point from their opening draw and face a **Tunisia** side sitting fourth on 0 points and already up against it after a 4-0 defeat — making this a must-win for the Carthage Eagles. The Samurai Blue's quick, technical passing game will test Tunisia's defensive discipline, but after **Netherlands**' five-goal hammering of **Sweden** earlier today, Japan know a win here would put them right back in contention for the knockouts.
